16.reason: [The exponent should be -(cnt), but the count starts at 1 and includes the first non-zero digit position incorrectly. Also, if all digits after decimal are zero (which shouldn't happen per problem statement), this would access out-of-bound memory. However, the main logical error is that 'cnt' counts leading zeros plus one, so exponent should be -(cnt) but actually needs to be -(number of leading zeros + 1). The current 'cnt' initialization to 1 causes off-by-one error in exponent.]
